title: USGS Minerals Yearbook — The Mineral Industry of Iran (Volume III, Area Reports: International), full narrative reports + tables-only data releases
source_org: U.S. Geological Survey, National Minerals Information Center
download_url: https://pubs.usgs.gov/myb/vol3/{year}/myb3-{year}-iran.pdf (and .xlsx) — see files list for exact per-year URLs
time_coverage: 2016, 2017-18, 2019, 2020-21, 2022, 2023 (USGS country page also lists PDF reports back to 1994 and tables back to 2002, not all downloaded here — see notes)
frequency: annual (some years combined into biennial reports, e.g. 2017-18, 2020-21)
units: metric tons / other units as described per-table in each file; narrative PDFs also discuss mineral-sector share of GDP (%)
notes: Found via https://www.usgs.gov/centers/national-minerals-information-center/iran, which lists 'Reports' (full narrative PDF) for every year 1994-2023 and 'Tables' (data tables XLS/XLSX) for every year 2002-2023. Downloaded the 6 most recent years for both Reports and Tables as instructed (2016, 2017-18, 2019, 2020-21, 2022, 2023) — all verified as genuine PDF/Excel files via `file`, none are bot-challenge pages. The 2016 tables file is served as legacy .xls (not .xlsx) — this is the actual USGS-hosted filename/format, kept as-is. Older years (1994-2015 reports, 2002-2015 tables) exist at stable predictable URLs on the same country page (see raw href list captured during this session) and could be pulled in a follow-up if a longer back-run is wanted; not downloaded now to stay within 'recent years' scope per task instructions. USGS page confirms government-side facts consistent with our other Iran sources: MIMT oversees the mineral sector (issuing permits, promoting investment) and IMIDRO controls most large-capacity state mineral companies — both are separately covered by other datasets in this agent's task list (mimt-iran, imidro-iran).
